name: settings-picker
description: Exercises the anchored floating surface pattern: select triggers composed with anchored dropdown-menus (anchor + on-dismiss + menu-item), model-owned open state, exclusive open across two pickers.
prompt:
This workspace is a freshly scaffolded Native SDK native-frontend app (see README.md; the view is src/app.native, the logic src/main.zig). Replace the counter with a small appearance settings panel.

Requirements:
- A picker row labeled exactly "Theme": a closed picker control that always shows the currently selected theme's name. The options are exactly "System", "Light", and "Dark", starting on "System".
- Pressing the closed picker opens a floating menu listing all three options. The menu must float OVER the content below it, anchored to the picker — opening it must not push the rest of the layout down or reflow any sibling. The currently selected option is visually marked inside the open menu.
- Choosing an option makes it the current selection and closes the menu. Pressing Escape or clicking anywhere outside the open menu must close it without changing the selection.
- A second picker row labeled exactly "Accent" with options exactly "Blue", "Green", and "Orange" (starting on "Blue"), behaving the same way. At most one menu is open at a time: opening one picker closes the other.
- A status bar with the exact text "Theme: <theme> · Accent: <accent>" (so "Theme: System · Accent: Blue" at first), updating live with the selections.
- Keep all logic in src/main.zig (Model, Msg, update); the markup only binds values and dispatches messages.
- Validate the markup with `native markup check src/app.native`, and keep `zig build test` green — extend src/tests.zig so the tests cover opening a picker, choosing an option through the real dispatch path (assert the selection changed and the menu closed), and the dismiss path (the menu closes, the selection does not change).

When you are done, make sure `zig build test` passes and summarize what you built.

checks.description: the closed picker is a <select> trigger
the options float in an anchored <dropdown-menu>
live snapshot shows both closed pickers with their initial values and the summary

criteria: The picker composition follows the framework's anchored-surface design: the open state is a model-owned flag, the floating menu is mounted under a conditional and anchored to the trigger (no fake inline expansion that reflows the layout), and on-dismiss clears the flag model-side.

Model/Msg/update follow The Elm Architecture idiomatically: picking an option is one message that sets the value AND closes the menu, the exclusive-open rule (opening one picker closes the other) lives in update, and no derivable state is stored.
Tests are meaningful: they open a picker, pick an option, and dismiss through the real dispatch path, asserting both the selection and the open/closed state each time.
The markup stays declarative: two picker rows share their structure sensibly (template or clean repetition), all strings and state live in the model, no logic is smuggled into the view.
checks.description: quality of the anchored picker composition beyond compliance
